title: NOC 43100 – Elementary and Secondary School Teacher Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Elementary and Secondary School Teacher Assistants under NOC 43100 support classroom teachers in delivering educational programs and assisting students with academic, behavioural, and special needs requirements. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation requires formal post-secondary training and/or structured on-the-job experience. Teacher Assistants play a vital role in creating inclusive and supportive learning environments across Canada’s education system.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group431 – Assisting occupations in educationMinor Group4310 – Assisting occupations in educationNOC Code43100

duties: Assist students with integration into the classroom
Support students individually or in small groups
Reinforce learning concepts under teacher supervision
Assist students with special needs using:Sign languageBrailleRemedial learning techniques
Supervise students during field trips and school activities
Assist with audio-visual and classroom equipment
Help in school libraries or administrative offices
Monitor students during recess and lunch periods
employment_requirements: Completion of a 10-month college program in:Teaching assistanceEducational assistanceSocial science program
Specialized training required for assisting students with special needs
Experience working with children is highly valued
Some school boards may require background checks and additional certifications.
salary_prospects: Elementary and Secondary School Teacher Assistants typically earn between:
$30,800 – $57,200 per year,depending on province, school board, and experience.
Northern territories generally offer higher wages.
job_outlook: Increased support for students with disabilities
labour_market_demand: Opportunities in public and private schools
Meaningful community and youth engagement work
Teacher Assistants are especially needed in provinces with expanding school systems and special education programs.
pathways: Teacher Assistants fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
Express Entry (if eligible under skilled categories)
If you are planning to work as a Teacher Assistant in Canada, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your pathway to Canada PR.
